rule.ruleText: Each equipped item instance contributes 1 copy of each of its cards by default. A survivor can train a specific card from that specific item instance to add extra copies, up to 3 total copies of that card from that one item.
rule.duplicateItemRule: Duplicate items are tracked separately by gearInstanceId. Two identical weapons can each train their own copies, but each item instance is still capped at 3 copies of a given card.
rule.unequipRule: Extra copies granted by an item are removed from the deck immediately when that item is unequipped, destroyed, or no longer valid.

progressionSuggestion.copy2: Unlock the second copy after 3 successful plays of that card while the item is equipped, then pay 1 matching resource or 1 generic resource.
progressionSuggestion.copy3: Unlock the third copy after 6 total successful plays of that card while the item is equipped, then pay 1 rare/matching quarry resource or 2 generic resources.
progressionSuggestion.successfulPlayDefinition: The card resolves at least one meaningful effect: damage, block, healing, draw/filtering, status application, survival gain, resource gain, or party support.

rule.implementationReminder: Do not duplicate rows in the static card table for trained copies. Build the runtime deck by expanding cards according to each survivor's gearCardTraining state.

implementationNotes: Track training by survivorId + gearInstanceId + cardId, never just cardId.
Duplicate same-name weapons are legal but are separate item instances for card copy progression.
The static card database remains unique; runtime deck construction expands trained copies.
If an item is unequipped, all extra trained copies from that item leave the deck until it is re-equipped.
